hypothesis: If a generative model for Covalent Triazine Frameworks is pre-trained on large chemical databases (ChEMBL/ZINC), fine-tuned on a curated CTF dataset, and trained with reinforcement learning using domain-specific rewards (flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric hindrance, pattern matching), then it will produce CTF structures with higher validity, uniqueness, internal diversity, novelty, and expert-filter pass rates and lower Fréchet ChemNet Distance than configurations lacking these transfer-learning components.
